feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Community
feed.subtitle: SAP Community blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: The Joule Effect: Event Recap ⚡️
entry.content:
Last week at the SAP Casablanca office, we brought together SAP professionals and enthusiasts for our event The Joule Effect, a focused session on the future of Business AI.

With the support of the SAP Community team, and contributions from @Salah2  and @belahmew, we explored what SAP Joule really means for our ecosystem.

Key Takeaways

  • AI is changing the game: Faster, simpler SAP interactions
  • Clean Core is essential: The foundation for scalable and secure AI adoption
  • Consultants remain key: Technology evolves, but expertise drives real transformation

Beyond the Content

What stood out most was the exchange between experienced professionals and emerging SAP talents. This is exactly what we want to build in Casablanca: a space to learn, connect, and grow together.

What’s Next

This is just the start. Our goal is to establish a consistent SAP knowledge hub in Morocco.

If you missed it:

  • Follow the Casablanca tag on SAP Community
  • Connect with us on LinkedIn
  • Share what topic we should cover next

Thank you to everyone who made this first step a success. More is coming.

entry.title: Wollongong Stammtisch Recap Mar '26
entry.content:
The Wollongong Stammtisch has earned its place in the calendar with another fun meetup.  Once again we had a roughly even split of locals and Sydneysiders.  We were excited to meet Pranoti and Nalla for whom this was their first Stammtisch. We hope to see them at the next Sydney event, which will likely be in May

John & OnurThirroul BeachPaul, Mark, Pranoti & NallaSimon and MeGraham & SimonGlen & Scott

entry.title: Hello SAP Community! My name is Lakshmi Triveni and I am excited to start my journey.
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community!

My name is Sandireddy Lakshmi Triveni, and I’m a final-year Computer Science student at Chalapathi Institute of Engineering and Technology.I am a SAP Certified Associate with hands-on experience in SAP MM configuration, procure-to-pay cycle execution, and integration with FI and SD modules. Alongside my technical journey, I’ve also achieved a 357-day Duolingo streak, which reflects my persistence and commitment to daily learning.

As part of the Hello SAP Community Initiative, I’m excited to share my experience. You can read more about the initiative in Hello SAP Community Initiative , which gives the full background and context.

What is your professional life dream?

My professional dream is to become a skilled SAP consultant who can bridge the gap between business requirements and technical solutions. I want to empower organizations by simplifying complex SAP processes into clear, practical workflows that anyone can understand.

Where do you see yourself in 5 years?

In five years, I see myself leading SAP MM and S/4HANA implementation projects, mentoring juniors, and contributing actively to the SAP Community through blogs, tutorials, and presentations. I also envision myself expanding into AI-driven SAP solutions, combining my certification in Generative AI with enterprise applications.

What is your hobby?

My favorite hobby is reading books. I love immersing myself in different perspectives and stories, which not only relaxes me but also inspires creativity and fresh ideas in my professional work. Reading helps me stay curious and continuously expand my knowledge.

Share a fun fact/story about yourself that people don’t know

A fun fact about me is that I’m very disciplined when it comes to learning. For example, I’ve maintained a 357-day Duolingo streak without missing a single day. It started as a small challenge, but now it’s a symbol of persistence and consistency in my life qualities I also bring into my SAP journey.

What was the most fun project you ever participated in and why?

The most fun project I worked on was configuring the SAP MM pricing procedure. At first, the steps and counters felt confusing, but once I understood how condition types and schema assignments worked, everything clicked into place. I enjoyed troubleshooting errors and documenting each step in a clear, beginner-friendly way. This project gave me confidence and reinforced my passion for simplifying SAP concepts for learners.

 

My SAP TechEd Bangalore Experience

This year I had the opportunity to attend SAP TechEd Bangalore 2026, and it was truly inspiring. Being surrounded by professionals, students, and experts from across the SAP ecosystem gave me a sense of belonging to a global community. I attended sessions on SAP S/4HANA innovations, AI integration, and sustainability in enterprise systems, which broadened my perspective on how technology is shaping the future of businesses.

One of the highlights was interacting with mentors and peers who shared practical insights into SAP MM and its integration with other modules. The hands-on workshops helped me connect theory with real-world applications, and I left with renewed motivation to keep learning and contributing.

And now let’s hear from you!

Which 5 things do you absolutely want to achieve in life? Share your thoughts in the comments . I’d love to read them!

entry.title: Hello SAP Community! My name is Purnima and I'm Passionate About Building My Career in SAP
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community!

I’m Purnima, a B.Tech graduate from Chalapathi Institute of Technology, currently working on an SAP FICO implementation project.

 

Through this experience, I am developing strong knowledge in Financial Accounting (FI) and Controlling (CO), working with areas such as General Ledger, Accounts Payable, Accounts Receivable, and Cost Center Accounting. I am actively involved in configuration, testing, and business process analysis.

I enjoy understanding how SAP supports financial operations, enhances cost control, and enables data-driven decision-making. I am excited to further build my expertise in SAP FICO.

 

Here are the questions I’ve chosen to answer as a part of the "Hello SAP Community" initiative.

What is your professional life dream?

My professional dream is to become a skilled SAP FICO consultant who can help organizations manage their financial processes efficiently. I aim to contribute to business growth by providing accurate financial insights and improving decision-making through SAP solutions.

Where do you see yourself in 5 years?

In the next 5 years, I see myself as an experienced SAP FICO consultant with strong expertise in implementation and support projects. I aim to work on real-time business scenarios, handle complex requirements, and continuously upgrade my skills in SAP and related technologies.

What is your hobby?

My hobbies include learning new technologies, improving my communication skills, and exploring SAP-related concepts. I also enjoy spending time enhancing my knowledge, which helps me grow both personally and professionally.

What is the best lesson your parents taught you?

The best lesson my parents taught me is the importance of hard work, discipline, and consistency. They always encouraged me to stay focused on my goals, be patient during challenges, and never give up. This mindset helps me stay motivated and continuously improve in my career.

Which 5 things do you absolutely want to achieve in life?

  1. To become a successful SAP FICO consultant with strong expertise in financial processes
  2. To continuously learn and upgrade my skills in SAP and new technologies
  3. To achieve financial independence and stability
  4. To contribute to organizational success through my knowledge and work
  5. To maintain a good balance between my personal and professional life.

My SAP TechEd Bangalore Experience

This year, I had the incredible opportunity to attend SAP TechEd Bangalore 2026, and it was truly a rewarding experience. The event brought together developers, architects, and technology enthusiasts from across the ecosystem, creating a vibrant space for learning and collaboration.


From insightful keynote sessions to hands-on workshops, every moment was packed with value. I particularly enjoyed exploring the latest innovations in AI, cloud technologies, and SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). The live demos and real-world use cases helped bridge the gap between theory and practical application.

One of the most valuable aspects was networking—connecting with like-minded professionals, SAP experts, and industry leaders. These conversations opened new perspectives and ideas that I’m excited to take forward in my work.

 

And now let’s hear from you!

I would love to hear your thoughts.

What is your personal life dream?

Feel free to share your answer in the comments and tell us about your goals and dreams.

entry.title: Hello SAP Community! My name is Kavuri Revathi and I'm excited to begin my journey in the SAP world.
entry.content:
My name is Kavuri Revathi and I'm student at Chalapathi Institute of Engineering And Technology in Guntur, Andhra Pradesh, India.

I am currently pursuing my B.Tech final year in Computer Science and Engineering. Along with my academic studies, I have completed my certification in SAP End-to-End Business Process. This helped me understand how different business functions like procurement, inventory management, and finance are integrated within SAP systems.

Here are the questions I’ve chosen to answer as a part of the "Hello SAP Community" initiative:

What is your professional life dream?

My career goal is to become a skilled SAP consultant who can help organizations improve their business processes and efficiency. I want to work on real-time projects and contribute to digital transformation by using SAP solutions.

What is your Hobby?


My hobbies include travelling, learning new technologies and improving my technical skills . I enjoy exploring SAP concepts, practicing different business scenarios, and enhancing my problem-solving abilities. I also like reading about emerging technologies and how they impact industries.

What is the best lesson you have learned so far?

One of the most important lessons I have learned is the value of continuous learning and consistency. In the field of technology, staying updated and practicing regularly is key to success. This mindset helps me stay motivated and focused on my goals.

My SAP TechEd Bangalore experience

Recently, I had the opportunity to attend the SAP TechEd event in Bangalore, and it was truly an incredible experience for me. It was inspiring to meet SAP experts and senior professionals and see how humble and approachable they were while interacting with participants.

I also got the chance to connect with people from different organizations, which helped me understand the wide range of opportunities available in the SAP ecosystem. Through these interactions, I learned how companies are adopting new technologies like Artificial Intelligence and Cloud to transform their business processes.

During the event, I also appeared for the Generative AI certification exam at the center. Even though there were a few technical issues, the support team handled the situation very calmly and professionally, which made the experience smooth and comfortable.

The sessions and speaker presentations were highly insightful. Overall, this experience motivated me to continue learning and building my career in SAP and emerging technologies.

And now let’s hear from you!

I would love to hear your thoughts.

What is the best lesson you have learned?

Feel free to share your answer in the comments and tell us about your goals and dreams.

entry.title: SAP Community Hobbies Series : [Not Just a Hobby: A Story of Small Classrooms and Big Dreams]
entry.content:
Hi, My name is Abhijna,  I am  an engineering student and I would like to share about my journey of teaching and connecting with young minds as part of the SAP Community Hobbies series.  I am a student with a deep curiosity for connections in all forms. Whether it’s understanding how data travels through networks or building meaningful connections with people, I find myself drawn to both worlds equally. With a strong interest in electronics and networking, I enjoy exploring how systems work, and at the same time, how small human interactions can create lasting impact. And somewhere between these two, I found a journey that’s just getting started.

Coming from a family where teaching has always been more than just a profession, I grew up witnessing the quiet impact it can create. Among all those influences, my grandfather has been my biggest inspiration. Not because of titles or achievements, but because of the way people still remember him, with respect, gratitude, and stories of how he shaped their lives..

About a year or two ago, I visited a  school, one where my grandfather had once taught. I didn’t go there with a plan. It was just a visit. But what I experienced there stayed with me. The respect people had for him, the way they remembered him, and the number of lives he had impacted, it made me pause. It made me think. What if I could become even a small reason for someone to move forward in life?

That thought stayed. And eventually, it turned into action. I went back not as a visitor this time, but as someone who wanted to give. I started small. I spent a week teaching dance, just to connect. And then I began talking to students about things they usually don’t get exposed to, what engineering is, what opportunities exist, what lies beyond what they see every day.

And that’s when I truly understood something. There is a different kind of energy in  school classrooms. The students there don’t just sit and listen , they want to learn. There is curiosity, there is respect, and there is this quiet hunger for knowledge. They are waiting for someone to show them what’s possible.

And being able to stand there, even for a short time, felt like a responsibility , and also a privilege. Of course, it wasn’t always easy.

Standing in front of students who are just a few years younger than you makes you question yourself. Will they understand? Will this make sense to them? Especially when you’re trying to explain concepts like electronics or embedded systems, things that even we find complex. But over time, I realized something simple. If you can relate knowledge to life, anyone can understand it. When I started breaking things down into examples they could see and feel , like explaining sensors through real-life situations , their response changed completely. It wasn’t confusion anymore. It was curiosity. One moment, in particular, stays with me. At the end of a session, a 7th-grade student came up to me and confidently named two sensors we had discussed. Just two. But that moment meant everything. Because it showed me that something had stayed with him. And sometimes, that’s all it takes.

A small understanding. A small spark. A small beginning.

There are many misconceptions around  schools, that it’s difficult to engage, that students may not be interested, or that opportunities to contribute are limited. But my experience has been the opposite. When approached in the right way, schools are open. And the students? They are ready. All they need is someone to show up.

When I think about how this connects to the SAP world, I see a strong parallel. SAP is all about creating impact through structured systems, data, and processes. Similarly, what I do involves identifying gaps, organizing knowledge, and delivering it in a way that creates meaningful outcomes. It’s about enabling growth, improving accessibility, and making information useful ,just like SAP does, but in a human, grassroots way.

This journey has also changed the way I look at learning. To teach even a little, I’ve realized I need to keep learning constantly. It’s not a one-way process. It’s a cycle , where giving and learning go hand in hand. And somewhere along the way, this stopped feeling like just a hobby. It became something more personal. Something that connects who I am, where I come from, and what I want to become. Because at the end of the day, it’s not always about doing something big. Sometimes, it’s just about showing up in a small classroom , and becoming a reason for someone to dream bigger.

entry.title: Hello SAP Community! My name is Angel Aradhana, and I am excited to begin my journey on SAP.
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community!

My name is Angel Aradhana , and I’m currently pursuing Mechanical Engineering at REVA University in Bangalore, India.

I am very happy to be part of the Hello SAP Community initiative. This opportunity allows beginners like me to explore the SAP ecosystem and connect with professionals around the world.

As a student, I am interested in learning new technologies and understanding how SAP solutions help businesses manage their operations efficiently. Through the SAP Community, I hope to learn from experts, discover new ideas, and grow my professional network.

I look forward to sharing my learning journey and contributing to the  SAP  Community in the future.

Thank you SAP Community for this wonderful opportunity!

 

 

Here are the questions I’ve chosen to answer as a part of the "Hello SAP Community" initiative.

1. What is your professional life dream?

My professional dream is to build a successful career in technology and engineering. I want to continuously learn new skills and work on innovative solutions that help organizations improve their efficiency. I also aim to contribute to the tech community and grow as a professional in the global technology ecosystem.

2. Where do you see yourself in 5 years?

In the next five years, I see myself as a skilled professional in the technology industry. I aim to develop strong technical knowledge, gain hands-on experience, and contribute to real-world projects. I also aspire to continuously learn, grow professionally, and make a meaningful contribution to my organization and community

3. What is your hobby?

One of my hobbies is learning about new technologies and exploring new ideas related to engineering and innovation. I also enjoy reading, learning new skills online, and improving my knowledge through different educational platforms.

4. Which 4things do you absolutely want to achieve in life?

There are several goals I want to achieve in my life:

Build a successful professional career.
Continuously learn and develop new skills.
Contribute to society through knowledge and innovation.
Inspire others through my journey and experiences.


5. What is the best lesson your parents taught you?

The best lesson my parents taught me is the importance of hard work, honesty, and patience. They always encouraged me to stay focused on my goals and never give up during difficult situations. These values continue to guide me in my education and future career.

Before I end this post, here’s something I’m really curious about:

If you could master one SAP technology or module, which one would it be and why?

 

 

 

#HelloSAPcommunity #SAPcommunity #SAPLearning #SAPForBeginners #TechCommunity #FutureWithSAP

entry.title: Hello SAP Community! My name is Papasani Thrisha and excited to Start my SAP Learning journey!
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community! I’m Papasani Thrisha, a final-year BTech student in Computer Science and Engineering at Chalapathi Institute of Engineering and Technology. I’m fascinated by how businesses manage operations, supply chains, and enterprise processes using SAP solutions.
During my studies, I have explored various SAP concepts, including SAP ERP, SAP S/4HANA, and business process management. I enjoy learning how these technologies can improve efficiency, streamline operations, and enable data-driven decision-making.

Where do you see yourself in 5 years?

In five years, I hope to work with global organizations as an SAP professional, gaining expertise in multiple modules and contributing to impactful digital transformation projects.

Hobbies and interests
Apart from my professional interests, I enjoy reading about emerging technologies, exploring enterprise systems, and staying updated on industry trends.  I enjoy exploring new places and also like spending time on sports, music, which helps me maintain a balanced lifestyle.

What is the best lesson my parents taught me? 

Among all the lessons my parents shared with me, the most impactful one was to respect every individual equally.

They taught me that true character is reflected not in how we treat people who are above us, but in how we treat everyone—regardless of their role, background, or identity.

This perspective has deeply influenced my mindset. It has encouraged me to look beyond external differences and value people for who they are.

In many ways, this lesson has shaped not just my behavior, but also my outlook on life—helping me become more empathetic, respectful, and mindful in my interactions.

What is my personal life dream? 

My personal life dream is closely connected with both gratitude and growth.

On a personal level, I aspire to take my parents on trips across the country and around the world—especially my mother, who truly loves to travel. Being able to give them those experiences would mean a lot to me, as they have always supported me throughout my journey.

From a professional perspective, I dream of building a strong career in the SAP ecosystem, where I can contribute to creating intelligent and impactful enterprise solutions. I want to be part of a journey where technology—especially SAP, AI, and cloud—helps businesses grow while also improving people’s everyday lives.

Another dream that is very close to my heart is to give back to society. I hope to use both my personal efforts and professional success to contribute meaningfully—such as providing meals to old age homes and orphanages, and supporting initiatives that create real social impact.

For me, success is not just about achieving career milestones in SAP, but also about using that success to create meaningful experiences for my family and make a positive difference in society.

What do I imagine or predict is the future of AI?

I imagine a future where AI becomes a natural part of our everyday lives.

In daily life, AI could help manage traffic without signals, make homes smarter by cleaning automatically, and even create products that adjust to our needs, like smart footwear.

In the world of technology and SAP, AI will help businesses work more efficiently by automating tasks, analyzing data, and supporting better decision-making.

Overall, I believe AI will make life easier, faster, and more efficient, while helping both individuals and organizations grow.

My SAP TechEd Bangalore Experience

Attending SAP TechEd in Bangalore was a truly inspiring experience.

It gave me the opportunity to interact with industry experts, attend hands-on sessions, and explore how SAP is integrating AI, cloud, and modern technologies into enterprise solutions.

One of the most memorable aspects was the supportive and approachable nature of the SAP community. I also had the opportunity to attempt a Generative AI certification during the event, which was a great learning experience.

 

 The event also gave me the opportunity to expand my network, learn directly from industry experts, and gain a clearer perspective on the future of enterprise technology. 

 I was also introduced to the SAP Community, a platform where individuals share not only technical knowledge but also their hobbies, interests, and personal experiences. It was Moshe Naveh who introduced a group of us students to this community and encouraged us to actively contribute. His encouragement inspired me to write my first SAP Community blog, where I shared my thoughts on emerging technologies and their impact on society.

Attending SAP TechEd and being part of the SAP Community has been an incredible learning experience. I gained hands-on insights into emerging technologies, learned from industry experts, and connected with like-minded peers.

This journey has inspired me to continue exploring SAP, contribute to the community, and apply what I learn to real-world challenges.

And now let’s hear from you!

I would love to hear your thoughts.

What is your personal life dream? 

I would love to hear your thoughts—feel free to share them in the comments!

entry.title: SAP Stammtisch Plus - Pune (March 2026) Recap
entry.content:
What started as a plan to bring the community back together turned into a morning full of ideas, questions, and some very honest conversations around AI in the SAP space. Safe to say, the energy in the room did not disappoint! 🙌

A quick look at what we covered:

• From AI Experiments to an Enterprise AI Operating System
A strong reminder that AI success is not about more models, but better data and clearer foundations. Moving from experiments to real enterprise adoption needs structure.

• AI-Powered Extensions on SAP BTP
A very real example of cleaning and optimizing large-scale master data using a multi-agent approach. If there was one takeaway here, it’s this: bad data still causes most problems.

• ITOps Copilot
AI stepping into IT operations with LLM-driven ticket analysis. Faster insights, less manual effort, and a glimpse into how support teams might work going forward.

• SAP Document AI in Action
Invoices going from paper to structured SAP entries with minimal human intervention. Simple idea, big impact.

• The Future of Intelligent ERP
AI, Joule, and the “right data, right process, right time” approach shaping what the next generation of ERP could look like.

But beyond the sessions, what really stood out was the community itself. Conversations that continued between sessions, people exchanging notes, and a room that stayed engaged throughout.

Also, putting this together was just as much fun as attending it. Always a good sign! 💪

A big thank you to our speakers, participants, and everyone who helped make this happen behind the scenes.

We’re just getting started with Stammtisch Plus in Pune. More to come! Stay tuned!

Scroll for some glimpses of the event!

Organizers & Volunteers:

  • @Parineeta_Somvanshi 
  • @Punith_Oswal 
  • @Asutosh21 

#StammPune

Best Regards,
SAP Community Group – Pune 


Our Speakers!Our Volunteers!

entry.title: Focus of this Learning Group and Best Practices for Using the Forum
entry.content:
In this Learning Group we want to connect learners in order to exchange ideas and to provide mutual support on learning tasks in the Supply Chain area. 
Hence, we only want to address questions related to the training content given on Learning.SAP.com and SAP Learning Hub. 

When you post a question or start a discussion, it is both helpful and speeds up things for all involved if you mention the course ID, course title, collection (if known), unit/lesson/exercise/step (as applicable) – along with your query. This makes it way easier for both other learners and moderators/dispatchers to understand, classify, reproduce, and forward the item to the respective experts, if necessary. 

Note that for very different reasons, we can’t cover any consulting issues, though. If your question is related to a concrete consulting problem, we'd recommend not losing valuable time and putting this question to our Consulting Communities instead: https://community.sap.com/topics/supply-chain-management 

Thank you for your understanding!

entry.title: 🌍 Comunidade SAP Joinville esta crescendo. Sejam Bem Vindos!
entry.content:
Queremos agradecer a chegada dos novos membros à Comunidade SAP Joinville 🙏

A nossa comunidade é construída por pessoas.
E é isso que faz ela crescer, ganhar relevância e gerar valor real.

Se você chegou agora, aproveite para se apresentar nos comentários:
o que você faz hoje? em qual área do SAP atua ou quer atuar?

👉 Compartilhe suas experiências
👉 Traga aprendizados do dia a dia
👉 Interaja com outros profissionais

Temos recebido pedidos para criação de grupos externos, mas acreditamos que a força da comunidade está aqui.
É aqui que conseguimos conectar pessoas, compartilhar conhecimento e gerar valor para todos.

A comunidade é construída por pessoas.
E é na troca que ela cresce e evolui.

#SAPCommunity #SITJoinville #SAPBrasil #SAPInsideTrack #ComunidadeSAP

entry.title: Accelerating SAP ICSM and IRT integration with pre-packaged sample content
entry.content:
Introduction

Integrating SAP Intelligent Clinical Supply Management (ICSM) with IRT/RTSM systems is essential for running efficient clinical trials but many teams still end up reinventing the same integration patterns.

To help the ecosystem move faster, sample integration content for ICSM is now available on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub, providing a practical starting point for building integrations between ICSM and IRT systems using SAP Integration Suite.

This post introduces the content, highlights the supported ICSM-IRT scenarios and explains how partners and customers can reuse and extend these building blocks to accelerate real‑world integrations.

Business value

Instead of starting from scratch, partners and customer can now:

  • Reuse proven integration patterns
  • Accelerate ICSM-IRT implementations
  • Focus on value‑adding extensions rather than plumbing
  • Align with SAP‑recommended architecture and standards

The content is intentionally modular, making it easier to adapt to different IRT systems or extend with additional scenarios.

What's Included

The published package SAP Intelligent Clinical Supply Management-Sample Integration with IRT demonstrates end-to-end integration scenarios across both planning and operations, based on GS1 Clinical Trials standards (3.6) and SAP-recommended integration patterns.

How to Consume the Accelerator Content

This can be consumed using SAP’s standard process for pre-packaged integration content: The pre-packaged integration content in SAP integration Suite is a set of Integration flows (iflows) leveraging the capabilities of SAP Integration Suite and SAP BTP to integrate IRT and SAP ICSM smoothly. This prepackage content takes care of many aspects of integration; some of these are as follows:

  1. Connect the IRT system to SAP Integration Suite artifacts via API management.
  2. Transformation of message payload from GS1 to ICSM and vice versa.
  3. Seamless integration of SAP Event Mesh to take an action against the events raised from ICSM (e.g. sales order created; Inventory report executed etc.).

This pre-package integration content supports the following interfaces for planning and operation in ICSM:

Interfaces for ICSM Planning

  1. Study Information
  2. IRT actuals and Master

Interfaces for ICSM Operations

  1. Inventory Report
  2. Shipment request and confirmation
  3. Dispatch Advice
  4. Receiving Advice
  5. Dispensation Advice
  6. Kit Status Change (Inbound/Outbound)

Below are the steps to consume the pre-packaged integration content:

Step 1 - Discover the package

Navigate to your SAP Integration Suite - Integration - Discover tab and search for the package "SAP Intelligent Clinical Supply Management Integration with IRT"

Below is the diagram shows the navigation steps.

                                                                                                              Fig: Integration Suite

Select the package and click copy to move it into your design workspace.  

Note: As with all standard packages, once you copy it, modifications in your workplace do not auto-inherit future SAP updates- SAP recommends keeping custom changes separate.

Step 2 - Review the Artifacts

Review each artifact integration flows and configure the user credentials for ICSM APIs and OAuth2 Client Credentials for event mesh using its service key values.

Step 3 - Configure the defined externalized parameters in each artifact

Update the values in each of the defined externalized parameters in each of the artifact integration flows.

The integration flows combine API-based and Event-driven approaches using SAP Integration Suite, SAP API Management, SAP Event Mesh and Cloud Connector, reflecting a reference architecture suitable for real-world landscapes.

Reference, not a Black Box

This is sample/accelerator content. Its purpose is to illustrate integration approaches and provide reusable building blocks - not to replace product features.

As with other SAP community assets, it does not come with the same lifecycle guarantees or support rigor as standard SAP product functionality. Instead, it is meant to enable the ecosystem to innovate, customize, and deliver differentiated solutions on top of SAP ICSM.

Get Started

  • Explore the sample Integration Package on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub.
  • Review the ICSM-IRT Integration architecture and patterns.
  • Copy, adapt and extend the content for your own scenarios.

If you are an IRT vendor, Partner or Customer working with ICSM, then this is an opportunity to reduce the integration effort and contribute to a growing, interoperable ecosystem around clinical supply management.

This Accelerator Content lays out the groundwork for real-world ICSM-IRT integrations by providing pre-built, GS1 aligned, event driven flows that can be directly adopted for the proof-of-concept work. 

In the upcoming blog of this series, this accelerator content forms the technical backbone for validating the end-to-end data exchange between ICSM and an IRT platform, which accelerates onboarding IRT partners and helps to reduce the custom development effort.

Engage with us

If you are interested in learning more about SAP Intelligent Clinical Supply Management, please consider following the new "SAP Intelligent Clinical Supply Management".

We are happy to receive your feedback, questions or thoughts about SAP Intelligent Clinical Supply Management in a comment.

entry.title: SAP Community Voice: SAP Champions and Mentor tips, new ABAP Keyword and upcoming SAP Inside Tracks!
entry.content:
Featured Blog Posts 

Fiori Elements Navigation Patterns 

SAP Champion Mio Yasutake published a valuable blog post last week, bringing light to Fiori Elements Navigation Patterns Showcase. That are multiple ways to navigate Fiori Elements from one application to another, While the SAPUI5 documentation explains each annotation in detail, it is often focused on code snippets. Which can be difficult sometimes to visualize how each navigation pattern behaves in the UI. To address this, Mio created a repository that demonstrates many Fiori Elements navigation patterns along with their actual UI behavior. On her blog post, you will find the link for the sample inside Github and her detailed explanation on how navigation is initiated and how data is passed to the target app. The repository allows developers to explore real examples, compare patterns like semantic links and intent-based navigation, and follow a simple decision guide to choose the right approach for their scenario. 

Read Mio Yasutake Blog post here 

Essential health checks for SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition and Joule 

SAP UX specialist Leslie Castle shares in his latest blog post a set of essential health checks for SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition and Joule, helping ensure your setup is fully functional before onboarding business users. The guide walks through key areas such as architecture validation, principal propagation, content exposure, identity provisioning, and troubleshooting tools, offering step-by-step checks to confirm everything is correctly configured. Performing these checks will allow you to become more confident and leverage the significant benefits of Joule. With real examples and clear testing scenarios, the post helps teams quickly identify and resolve issues, so they can confidently roll out Joule and start leveraging its full business value from day one. 

Read Leslie Castle Blog post here 

OData MCP Proxy 

SAP Mentor Wouter Lemaire, introduces the OData MCP Proxy: an open-source, configuration-driven server that exposes any OData V2 or REST API as MCP (Model Context Protocol) tools, making them instantly accessible to AI assistants like Claude, Cursor, or any MCP-compatible client. In his blog post, Wouter how this proxy runs seamlessly on SAP BTP, leveraging services like Destination Service and XSUAA for secure, scalable integration. He shows in detail the architecture, the whole configuration file structure, transport modes, and how AI agents can intelligently interact with SAP services. In the end of his post, you will find a list five MCP servers he built using this exact pattern, for a different SAP service each. We highly recommend looking into all his blog posts in this series, plus the links he shared for GitHub! 

Read Wouter Lemaire Blog post here 

Brand-new ABAP Keyword 

The last blog post highlight was written by SAP expert Kilian Kilger announcing a brand-new top level ABAP keyword in ABAP CE 2602. Designed to fully leverage the power of SAP HANA, the new “MERGE” command combines multiple database operations, such as INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE-into a single, efficient statement. MERGE is a standard SQL database command. It exists on the HANA database and most other commercial databases. The post explains not only how “MERGE” works conceptually and through practical examples, but also the challenges of introducing new keywords in ABAP, including compatibility with existing macros. Due to its ability to join an internal or database table (or a global temporary table for that matter), it can be a very powerful tool in the hands of an ABAP developer.  You may find additional information in the SAP help article here.  

Read Kilian Kilger Blog post here 

Community News 

Ask me Anything with SAP Champions: Session 2 

In the past editions of the Community Voice, we have mentioned the amazing initiative from the SAP Champions program, the Ask me Anything series, and we are happy to let everyone know that the submission of questions for Session 2 is open! SAP Champion Marietta Lopes is leading the March edition, with more than 20 years of experience in SAP transformation programs, SAP S/4HANA implementations, enterprise architecture, governance frameworks, and digital innovation within the SAP ecosystem. This can be a great opportunity to get amazing advice from her on e.g. Clean Core in SAP Cloud ERP (Private & Public), RISE or GROW with SAP, Clean Core Governance & Methodologies and much more. To participate, you can either drop your question on the comments section of her blog post linked below or ask anonymously in the link here. 

Ask me Anything with SAP Champion Marietta 

Hello SAP Community: Blog Series in Beginners Corner 

March also marks the beginning of our new blog post series, the Hello SAP Community! Launched by the SAP Community Advocate Moshe Naveh, the initiative is a way of bringing light to the next generation of SAP professionals, creating a space where they can share more about them, what they have been studying, topics of interest, their vision of the future, how they grow in the era of AI and much more. The initiative has already started inside the Beginners Corner Group at SAP Community, with students from the Bangalore area. Every week the participants will post introduction blog posts about themselves that will allow us to get to know them, learn from them and connect. They will answer preset questions, and every participant will also add their own unique touch, insights and experiences.  Are you interested in participating? Make sure to check Moshe post, to find all the useful links, the preset questions and his contact for more information. 

Hello Community Blog Series  

Hobbies Series: Teaching in a School 

This week's Hobbies Series highlight is by the SAP Community member and engineering student, Abhijna blog post. She shares the story of how she started teaching and learning alongside young students, inspired by her grandfather’s impact as an educator. Two years ago she returned to the school where he was a teacher, with a desire to give back. What started with small interactions, like teaching dance and introducing basic concepts of engineering, quickly turned into meaningful moments of curiosity and connection. Through examples and simple explanations, she discovered that even complex topics can spark understanding when approached the right way. Her experience is a reminder that sometimes, creating impact isn’t about doing something big, but about showing up, sharing knowledge, and becoming a small reason for someone to dream bigger. We highly recommend checking her full story, and maybe you also feel inspired to spread your knowledge with more people around you 

Hobbies Series Abhijna Blog post here 

Events 

March Meet-up Recaps 

March was an amazing month filled with community-driven events all over the world. We would like to highlight some of the great recaps of location group members have shared:  

Starting with SAP Inside Track Joinville in Brazil, which took place on March 14 and was completely full of attendees! With a great recap shared by event organizer Gislaine, the event featured more than 7 sponsors and proved to be a big success. In case you also participated in the event, make sure to share your pictures and feedback in the comment section of Gislaine post or publish your own blog post in the Joinville group. 

Read SIT Joinville recap here 

Jumping to Casablanca, local group organizer Younes Elmir shared highlights from The Joule Effect event held last week at the SAP Casablanca office. Focused on AI topics, Younes described that what stood out most was the exchange between experienced professionals and emerging SAP talent. If you also would like to participate on the next events, you can join the Casablanca group and keep up with the updates. 

Read Casablanca event recap here 

Pune Stammtisch also took place last week, bringing local SAP community members together to discuss topics such as AI in the SAP space, Future of Intelligent ERP and much more. Event organizer Parineeta Somvanshi shared more details about the sessions in his latest recap, but for him the most special aspect was the great conversations that continued between sessions, people exchanging ideas and staying engaged throughout the event. If you are interested on the next upcoming events, or would like to share your own recap impressions, make sure to join the Pune group.  

Read Pune event recap here 

SAP Inside Track Sydney: Save the date 

Talking about opportunities to meet-up, we are happy with the announcement that the SAP Inside Track Sydney dates is finally out! On Friday, September 4, 2026, the event will happen at a new venue, for an elevated experience but with the same community spirit. SAP Champion Mustafa Bensam published the Save the Date on the events calendar page and soon he will publish more details including registration, call for speakers, and sponsorship opportunities. Stay tuned and don´t miss out in the opportunity to meet other Sydney group members, share knowledge and connect. 

SIT Sydney event post here 

SAP Inside Track Recife: Call out for Speakers 

Another great upcoming SIT is the SAP Inside Track Recife, that will happen on May 23 at the Hotel Manibu, in Boa Viagem. Since its first edition in 2026, this year the local group celebrates 10 years of SIT Recife and establishing itself as more than just a tech event. It’s a movement centered around people, experiences, and the continuous growth of the SAP ecosystem in Brazil. The registration for tickets and the agenda will soon to be announced.  But we would like to remind everyone that the Call for Speakers is open until April 10, and you just need to submit your proposal including topic, title and a short abstract to the email: recife@sitbrazil.com. Beyond the event announcement, SAP Champion Marssel Vilaca also have published blog posts with more details on how to apply for speaking and how to become a sponsor.  Check out each one of the posts inside Recife group to find out more details on how to support. 

SIT Recife event post here

entry.title: Introducing additional learning groups on SAP Community!
entry.content:
The best learning does not happen alone - it happens together.

To support these connections, we've just launched additional learning groups - your way on SAP Community to connect, stay motivated, and make real progress toward your learning goals. These groups are open to all learners, across topics, levels, and goals.

What you get when you join:

Every learning group offers a structured, engaging experience built to support your success:

  • Focused discussions in relation to courses and learning journeys on the SAP Learning site
  • Guided discussion prompts to deepen your knowledge
  • Peer insights and shared experiences you can learn from immediately
  • Built for every learner, whether you are starting something new, leveling up your skills or returning to learning after a break.

Join and explore the new learning groups on SAP Community now!

entry.title: Втілюйте AI-стратегії в життя ще швидше з SAP BTP
entry.content:
Маємо круті новини для тих, хто працює з SAP BTP AI Best Practices. Тепер опанувати найкращі практики стало значно простіше!

Ми запустили офіційний плейлист на YouTube, де кожна рекомендація розібрана «на молекули» у відеоформаті. 🎥

Що всередині?

  • Від бізнес-сценаріїв до функціонального використання.

  • Практичні поради, як будувати та масштабувати AI-рішення.

  • Покрокові приклади, що ідеально доповнюють офіційну документацію.

Це ваш must-watch інструмент, щоб впроваджувати штучний інтелект на платформі SAP BTP відповідально, ефективно та професійно.

Готові до перегляду? ▶️ Офіційний плейлист на YouTube: https://imsap.co/6040C5tTs

📚 Повна колекція текстових Best Practices тут: https://imsap.co/6041C5tTt

entry.title: 🚀 SAP Mini Inside Track – Chennai | Coming April 2026
entry.content:
We are excited to announce that the SAP Chennai Community is gearing up to host an upcoming SAP Mini Inside Track (SIT) in April 2026! 🎉

This community‑driven event will bring together SAP professionals, consultants, developers, architects, students, and enthusiasts from across Chennai and nearby regions to collaborate, learn, and share knowledge.

📍 Date & Venue:
Details will be announced very soon — stay tuned!

🌟 What to Expect at SAP Mini Inside Track – Chennai

✅ Cutting‑Edge SAP Topics
Explore insightful sessions covering the latest in SAP technologies, innovations, and real‑world experiences — including S/4HANA, BTP, integrations, cloud, AI, and more.

✅ Knowledge Sharing by the Community
Learn from local SAP experts and practitioners across various organizations who will share practical use cases, lessons learned, and best practices.

✅ Networking & Collaboration
Connect with fellow SAP professionals, exchange ideas, expand your network, and be part of meaningful conversations within the SAP ecosystem.

✅ Open Community Platform
Bring your thoughts, questions, and experiences — this is a collaborative space built by the community, for the community.

🔔 Stay Connected

More information on event agenda, speakers, registration, date, and venue will be shared shortly through this page.

Follow the SAP Chennai Community Page and keep an eye out for updates — exciting times ahead! 🙌

Let’s come together to learn, connect, and grow the SAP ecosystem in Chennai.

#SAPMiniInsideTrack #SAPChennai #SAPCommunity #SAPEvents
#InsideTrack #SAPLearning #SAPNetworking #ChennaiTech #SAPProfessionals

 

Just say the word 👍

Regards

Prasath

entry.title: Запис вебінару "Відповідність рішень SAP вимогам українського законодавства та обліку"
entry.content:
Дякуємо всім, хто доєднався до вебінару «SAP для України: відповідність рішень SAP вимогам українського законодавства та обліку» в рамках березневих онлайн‑подій SAP Україна. Ця зустріч стала частиною серії заходів, яку ми присвятили хмарі, даним, AI та підтримці користувачів.

Що обговорили на вебінарі

Під час сесії ми показали, як отримати максимум від SAP Support та SAP for Me, зокрема:

  • інфраструктуру SAP Support: які інструменти й сервіси доступні клієнтам;

  • управління користувачами та роботу з обліковими записами в SAP for Me;

  • завантаження ПЗ, роботу з ліцензіями та ключовими параметрами системної адміністрації;

  • сповіщення, сервісні запити та роль Customer Interaction Center (CIC) у щоденній взаємодії із SAP.

Це практична сесія для тих, хто відповідає за підтримку користувачів, стабільність ландшафту SAP та ефективну роботу з інструментами підтримки.

Запис вебінару доступний

Якщо ви не встигли приєднатися наживо або хочете повернутися до окремих блоків, запис вебінару вже доступний онлайн.

Дивіться у зручний час, діліться лінком з колегами та використовуйте отримані інсайти, щоб зробити вашу взаємодію з SAP Support ще прозорішою та ефективнішою.

entry.title: Breaking into SAP BTP
entry.content:
How can I learn about SAP?

One of the questions asked at our last networking event came from a master’s student who is interested in learning about SAP technology. It was quite a simple one - “How can I learn about SAP?”. 

While simple, her question has a far more complex answer beyond just “It depends…”. SAP is enormous - different products, different technologies, different disciplines. With SAP’s 50+years of history of creating complex business systems, where do you even start?

For myself, I got into SAP in the same way as most people I’ve met seem to - my boss made me learn it. One day my 20-year-old self was working on an old SQR program when my manager walked in and told me I would be learning SAP and sent me to the office next door where an implementation of SAP 3.1h was ongoing. It was, of course, the first time I had heard of SAP (or for that matter that an implementation was ongoing) and on arrival I met the consultant team who taught me ABAP, which I then worked in for the next two decades.

But if you’re not blessed with accidentally stumbling into a life changing career, how can you learn SAP technologies and be reasonably confident that an employer will value the effort you’ve put into learning it? In this blog I’m going to try and give some pointers as to how this can be accomplished. Just to put some kind of scope on this blog, we’ll be concentrating on SAP BTP.

 

Step 1 - Understand what it is

SAP BTP is the foundation technology SAP has created to act as their integrated "platform-as-a-service" (PaaS). Its primary role is to enable the "clean core" architecture and move customers away from building applications directly inside the SAP core.

The Four Functional Pillars

SAP BTP is organized into four distinct areas, each of which fulfils a specific role.

  • Application Development: These are the tools for professional developers (using the CAP model and BAS) and business users (using SAP Build) to create applications.
  • Integration: The SAP Integration Suite is a set of tools that allow BTP to connect to both SAP and on-SAP systems. In here you’ll find things such as Event Mesh, Destination services, technical adaptors (OData, SOAP, Rest etc.) and everything else you’ll need to plug systems into the BTP landscape.
  • Data and Analytics: This is where raw data is transformed into real information. It includes SAP HANA Cloud for data storage and SAP Analytics Cloud for visualization.
  • Artificial Intelligence: SAP BTP is where Joule, SAP’s generative AI copilot, exists. It provides SAP’s pre-built AI services for document processing, sentiment analysis, and recommendation engines that are already embedded into business workflows.

To learn these in more depth than can be put on a blog post read the "Discover SAP Business Technology Platform" learning journey on the sap learning portal. It covers these four pillars and can be found here.

https://learning.sap.com/courses/discovering-sap-business-technology-platform-1?searchId=7d314e8c-19cc-4f14-97ff-0570f56ab26d&listPosition=1

 

Step 2 - Get your hands dirty

Setup a BTP trial account

Reading up about BTP is useful, but nothing beats practical experience. SAP has free BTP trial accounts where you can sign up to a 90-day trial. It provides access to the BTP Cockpit where you can enable services like SAP Build, SAP HANA Cloud, and Integration Suite.

https://www.sap.com/products/technology-platform/trial.html#:~:text=You%20can%20choose%20a%20tutorial,services%20in%20a%20test%20environment.

From my own experience, it’s not for the faint of heart or non-technical among us as you need to do a fair amount of work to configure the system and learn how it operates. However, if you want to learn BTP admin setup, technical skills and eventually start building applications – then this is for you.

 

Explore the SAP Discovery Center

In addition to the SAP learning portal, SAP has created the discovery center. In it you will find “missions” which are step-by-step projects that walk you through specific use cases. It’s fully searchable by topic and has quite a lot of useful content.

Looking up SAP Joule, for example, shows 16 missions ranging from getting started with SAP Build and Joule (using that trial account you’ve surely setup by now) to integrating SAP Joule with Microsoft Co-Pilot.

The link can be found here https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/

 

Step 3 - Understand what you want to do

SAP BTP is too broad for any one person to master. Pick a track based on your role – or the role that you want. A few examples are below. This is by no means an exhaustive list and you may see yourself fitting into a different category altogether.

Track

Tools

Core Skills

Developer

CAP (Cloud Application Programming), SAPUI5, Node.js, Java

Building side-by-side extensions for S/4HANA, understanding the clean-core approach.

Integrator

SAP Integration Suite (Cloud Integration, API Management)

Connecting SAP to non-SAP systems (e.g., Salesforce, ServiceNow).

Low-Code/No Code

SAP Build (Apps, Process Automation, Work Zone)

Creating apps and workflows without deep coding knowledge

Data & AI

SAP HANA Cloud, SAP Analytics Cloud, SAP AI Core

Managing enterprise data and deploying AI models.

A subject that came up during our discussions (and which tends to be overlooked) is that knowledge matters. A financial expert with a grounding in accounting will be able to create and utilise finance based low-code solutions far more efficiently than someone who is relying on the machine to do it for them.

This is not going to go away in the age of LLMs and AI. Someone who knows how a business process works or what the subject theory is will be able to spot hallucinations, ask far more pertinent questions and direct outcomes more efficiently and with better results. Don’t neglect the grounding knowledge that will allow you to both use and master these tools.

 Advanced Technical Skills

This blog could only be about technical tools and skills, and I’d likely be writing it forever.  But in the interest of creating a shortlist, here are a few of the technical skills that standout.

  • Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP): This is where SAP wants developers to concentrate their efforts in BTP development. Core Data Services (CDS) are crucial to define data models and learning this route. For those of you who want to eventually sell products directly on the SAP store, then here’s where you do it.
  • SAP Business Application Studio (BAS): This is the cloud-based IDE (successor to Web IDE) where most BTP development happens. It has replaced the SAP Web IDE and is the primary tool SAP has provided for creating those side-by-side extensions. If you are just starting, focus entirely on BAS and avoid Web IDE. The SAP BTP trial can be used to access it and use the CAP (Cloud Application Programming) model within BAS.
  • Joule-Powered Development The biggest shift I’ve seen in the last while is the new Agent Builder and Joule Studio integration. You can now use natural language within BAS to generate CAP (Cloud Application Programming) models, UI5 code, and even specialized "AI Agents" that automate business processes. While this is still in the early stages, I’m looking forward to seeing what SAP brings us with this toolset.

 

Step 4 - Certification

At TechEd 2025, we learned that the certification process that SAP have employed for decades was being turned on its head. Previously, a consultant had to memorise volumes of information and then sit a multi-choice exam on the topic. My own, perhaps controversial, opinion was that this approach only ever proved that the consultant could do an exam - not the job. I never valued certification for this reason.

SAP has transitioned to a more performance-based certification model. Here, the would-be consultant is given an open book exam with problems to solve. It reflects the real-world scenario where we have the entire internet, custom AI agents and Joule for Consultants at our fingertips, not to mention other experts on the team you can rely on. Someone who has gone through this very different approach to getting certified needs to be able to problem solve and rapidly adjust to new realities. This type of certification would certainly push someone higher on my list of potential candidates to hire.

Just to note, while many courses are free, a paid subscription to the Learning Hub is required for official certification attempts and access to "Live Access" training systems.

https://learning.sap.com/certifications?page=1

Ultimately there is no “one route” into SAP and it takes time and effort to become an expert in any one area. Hopefully, however, this blog post will give you some guidance on where to start and how to learn some useful skills that an employer will value.

entry.title: Migration Notice - Moving Forward Together: LAC, Spain & Portugal join Global SAP Community soon!
entry.content:
Dear community members from our regional group LAC, Spain and Portugal,

To strengthen collaboration and reduce duplicated efforts, we will be merging our regional community "Latin America & Caribbean, Spain and Portugal" into the recently created Global - SAP Community. Going forward, regional posts, discussions and events will take place in the global group directly so we can improve visibility, share knowledge more broadly, and create better synergies.

What will happen

Existing discussions, blogposts, and events from the regional group will be moved to the global group of SAP University Alliances & SAP Young Thinkers

Timing
We plan to start the migration in the coming weeks and expect the move to be completed asap.

How you can help or what to expect

Please follow or join the global group now so you don’t miss updates: Global - SAP Community

Questions or concerns

If you have any comments or if you need assistance, leave your message in the comments feature below or write to us via universityalliances@sap.com

Thank you for your cooperation as we make this transition to better serve the community.

Best regards,

SAP Academic Partnerships – LAC, Portugal and Spain

entry.title: Dark Mode Beta Phase Complete: See What's New
entry.content:
We are excited to announce that Dark Mode is now live as the Beta Phase is complete! 

Last year, after listening to your feedback, we launched Dark Mode in beta, giving you the chance to choose your preferred visual experience. 

Since then, you have shared suggestions on how to make this feature more polished and seamless. With that in mind, we are happy to introduce the following improvements: 

  1. Light/Dark Mode Toggle 

A new toggle replaces the temporary text-based switch, making it easier to switch between themes for a more personalized browsing experience.

 

  1. Dark Mode Improvements for DSA Reporting Form 

The "Report Inappropriate Content" form has been updated to ensure all table content is visible and readable in dark mode. Previously unreadable white-on-white elements now follow the correct dark theme styling. 

Why this matters?  

Dark Mode improves how you experience the SAP Community everyday:  

  • More comfortable browsing: Reduced eye strain, especially in low-light environments, makes it easier to stay engaged for longer.   
  • Greater control over your experience: The new toggle lets you instantly switch between light and dark modes, so you can choose what works best for you at any moment.   
  • Better readability where it counts: Improvements to key areas like the reporting form to ensure content is always clear and accessible, no matter the theme.
